title: Gradient Flow in a Deep Neural Network
description: A diagram illustrating the backward propagation of gradients through the layers of a deep network during optimization, showing how partial derivatives are calculated and chained via the backpropagation algorithm.
relationships: Forward pass computes predictions from input to output
Loss is calculated by comparing prediction (ŷ) to true label (y)
Gradient of loss w.r.t. output layer weights (∂L/∂W3) is computed first
Gradient is propagated backward through chain rule: ∂L/∂W2 = ∂L/∂h2 * ∂h2/∂W2
Process continues layer-by-layer to compute gradient for all parameters
Gradients are used by optimizer (e.g., SGD) to update weights and minimize loss
